summary: The first_proof_2 dataset contains 10 research-level mathematics problems, of which 6 (prob-01 through prob-05 and prob-07) have a resolved proof issue each, indicating strong traction. No issues remain open across the documented problems, while prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, and prob-10 lack resolution records and represent the primary remaining gaps. The uniform 'Proof Status' count of 100 issues per question (q1–q10) suggests an evenly distributed evaluation or annotation workload rather than differential activity.
problems: prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, and prob-10 have no recorded resolved or open proof issues, leaving their status unverified
Proof Status counts are uniform (100 per question), offering no signal to differentiate problem-level difficulty or progress
Resolved issue titles are truncated, limiting visibility into the actual proof content and verification scope
No open issues are tracked anywhere, which may indicate incomplete issue logging for unresolved problems rather than genuine completion
highlights: Six of ten problems (prob-01 to prob-05, prob-07) each have a fully resolved proof issue, a 60% documented resolution rate
Strong breadth of resolved topics: descriptive set theory, piecewise-linear geometry, weighted Bernoulli sums, k-dilation inequalities, unique ergodicity, and quasi-reduced words
Zero open issues across all documented problems indicates clean, low-backlog status for the resolved set

suggested_todos.title: Create and track proof issues for prob-06, prob-08, prob-09, and prob-10 to establish their current status
Audit prob-06/08/09/10 to determine whether absence of issues means unstarted or merely unlogged
Review the six resolved proofs for verification completeness given truncated titles
Clarify what the uniform 100-issue Proof Status counts represent and whether they map to solvability
Prioritize next-week effort on the four undocumented problems, starting with the most tractable based on field expertise
Expand truncated issue titles into full descriptions for the dataset record
